C09000036 12/29/1991 December 29, 1991 Note From Strobe Talbott to MKA

State Department FOIA record C09000036, dated 12/29/1991, is titled "December 29, 1991 Note From Strobe Talbott to MKA". The released text begins: "They confirm what had been my assumption anyway: the central allegation of the piece is utter, mischievous nonsense; and I was in the dark by inadvertence. They also agree that I should have been read into this matter in a timely way, both because of my responsibilities in D and also because of my more specific duties on Russia policy."

C09000054 4/28/1993 Proposed Message to President Yeltsin

State Department FOIA record C09000054, dated 4/28/1993, is titled "Proposed Message to President Yeltsin". The released text begins: "I have also attached a proposed response to Yeltsin (which I have reviewed with Strobe Talbott). The proposed message includes our views on a variety of the most important issues on the agenda with Russia."

C09000053 7/29/1993 July 29, 1993 Note From Strobe Talbott To Tony Lake

State Department FOIA record C09000053, dated 5/4/1994, is titled "Opening comments about last night's CNN". The released text begins: "BC asks about Karen Elliot House ST: She's a neocon, wife of Kahn, beaten up alot of us over years, close to WSJ editorial line. C09000057 9/30/1993 September 30, 1993 Letter To Tony

State Department FOIA record C09000057, dated 9/30/1993, is titled "September 30, 1993 Letter To Tony". The released text begins: "I've been concerned over the past little whi.le, certainly going back to the Great Grachev Near-Non-Drop-By, that we really did need to sit down and talk through with each other a couple of issues. But that's not going to be very easy."

C09000058 5/2/1994 YOUR APPEARANCE ON CNN TOMORROW

State Department FOIA record C09000058, dated 5/2/1994, is titled "YOUR APPEARANCE ON CNN TOMORROW". The released text begins: "You can use the occasion to assert our objectives, explain our strategy, review our accomplishments, and push back against our critics. Like most other periods of great change, this era comes at the end of a war."

State Department FOIA record C09000034, dated 9/25/1995, is titled "September 25, 1995 letter From Strobe Talbott To Elise Lewis with attachment". The released text begins: "Lewis: I am writing in enthusiastic support of Tom Donilon for Council membership. C09000006 10/12/1996 October 12, 1996 Note From Strobe Talbott To Chief

State Department FOIA record C09000006, dated 3/22/1996, is titled "March 22, 1996 Letter From Acting Secretary To Elaine Agnes McGovern". The released text begins: "McGovern: l'm writing to convey my regret and apologies for the confusion at the Retirement Ceremony this morning. 0ur reliance on computer lists can and does get us in trouble, as it did today. I did want to congratulate you on a job well done in a series of difficult posts around the world: Belgrade, Pretoria, Rabat, Nairobi, Libreville, New Delhi, Colombo and, fiiially, Vienna."

State Department FOIA record C09000006, dated 3/22/1996, is titled "March 22, 1996 Letter From Acting Secretary To Elaine Agnes McGovern". The released text begins: "McGovern: l'm writing to convey my regret and apologies for the confusion at the Retirement Ceremony this morning. 0ur reliance on computer lists can and does get us in trouble, as it did today. I did want to congratulate you on a job well done in a series of difficult posts around the world: Belgrade, Pretoria, Rabat, Nairobi, Libreville, New Delhi, Colombo and, fiiially, Vienna."

The released text begins: "Naturally, we have to be careful not to be in the position of urging individual countries, especially traditional neutrals, to join the Alliance. Our message, as you reflect, is that the doors are open to eligible countries that want to join."

 PDF
C09000006 8/6/1998 August 6, 1998 Note from Deputy Secretary

State Department FOIA record C09000006, dated 3/22/1996, is titled "March 22, 1996 Letter From Acting Secretary To Elaine Agnes McGovern". The released text begins: "McGovern: l'm writing to convey my regret and apologies for the confusion at the Retirement Ceremony this morning. 0ur reliance on computer lists can and does get us in trouble, as it did today. I did want to congratulate you on a job well done in a series of difficult posts around the world: Belgrade, Pretoria, Rabat, Nairobi, Libreville, New Delhi, Colombo and, fiiially, Vienna."

C09000054 8/7/1998 August 7, 1998 Note From Margie Soltis To Kristie Kenny w/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000054, dated 4/28/1993, is titled "Proposed Message to President Yeltsin". The released text begins: "I have also attached a proposed response to Yeltsin (which I have reviewed with Strobe Talbott). The proposed message includes our views on a variety of the most important issues on the agenda with Russia."

C09000057 8/16/1998 August 16, 1998 Note From Strobe Talbott To Amb. Eizenstat, E w/Attachment(s)

State Department FOIA record C09000057, dated 9/30/1993, is titled "September 30, 1993 Letter To Tony". The released text begins: "I've been concerned over the past little whi.le, certainly going back to the Great Grachev Near-Non-Drop-By, that we really did need to sit down and talk through with each other a couple of issues. C09000058 8/16/1998 August 16, 1998 Note From Strobe Talbott To Jim Foley, PA

State Department FOIA record C09000058, dated 5/2/1994, is titled "YOUR APPEARANCE ON CNN TOMORROW". The released text begins: "You can use the occasion to assert our objectives, explain our strategy, review our accomplishments, and push back against our critics. Like most other periods of great change, this era comes at the end of a war."

C09000059 8/16/1998 August 16, 1998 Note From Strobe Talbott To Amb. Sestanovich, S/NIS

State Department FOIA record C09000059, dated 10/19/1996, is titled "October 19, 1996 Letter From George To Strobe Talbott". The released text begins: "In accordance with rather trustworthy information, there is an agreement signed by US Assistant Secretary of Defense and Deputy Chief of Staff of the Turkish Army to modify Turkish F-16 aircrafts in order to give them nuclear weapons deliv… Specifically, it concerns a special certification of the above-mentioned aircrafts and the possibility of their deployment with nuclear equipment."

The released text begins: "Lewis: I am writing in enthusiastic support of Tom Donilon for Council membership. Please take this letter as a genuinely personal endorsement from someone who knows the Council well -- and of someone who, I'm convinced, would make a real contribution to the activities of the CFR for a long time to come."

C09000036 11/9/1998 Handwritten Letter

State Department FOIA record C09000036, dated 12/29/1991, is titled "December 29, 1991 Note From Strobe Talbott to MKA". The released text begins: "They confirm what had been my assumption anyway: the central allegation of the piece is utter, mischievous nonsense; and I was in the dark by inadvertence. They also agree that I should have been read into this matter in a timely way, both because of my responsibilities in D and also because of my more specific duties on Russia policy."

State Department FOIA record C09000006, dated 3/22/1996, is titled "March 22, 1996 Letter From Acting Secretary To Elaine Agnes McGovern". The released text begins: "McGovern: l'm writing to convey my regret and apologies for the confusion at the Retirement Ceremony this morning. 0ur reliance on computer lists can and does get us in trouble, as it did today. I did want to congratulate you on a job well done in a series of difficult posts around the world: Belgrade, Pretoria, Rabat, Nairobi, Libreville, New Delhi, Colombo and, fiiially, Vienna."

State Department FOIA record C09000034, dated 9/25/1995, is titled "September 25, 1995 letter From Strobe Talbott To Elise Lewis with attachment". The released text begins: "Lewis: I am writing in enthusiastic support of Tom Donilon for Council membership. The released text begins: "They confirm what had been my assumption anyway: the central allegation of the piece is utter, mischievous nonsense; and I was in the dark by inadvertence. C09000100 n/a Note To KD

State Department FOIA record C09000100, dated 9/4/1997, is titled "September 4, 1997 Note From Strobe Talbott To Richard Schifter". The released text begins: "Naturally, we have to be careful not to be in the position of urging individual countries, especially traditional neutrals, to join the Alliance. Our message, as you reflect, is that the doors are open to eligible countries that want to join."

C09000100 n/a Letter From To Mr. Foreign Minister

State Department FOIA record C09000100, dated 9/4/1997, is titled "September 4, 1997 Note From Strobe Talbott To Richard Schifter". The released text begins: "Naturally, we have to be careful not to be in the position of urging individual countries, especially traditional neutrals, to join the Alliance. Our message, as you reflect, is that the doors are open to eligible countries that want to join."
